ticket.subject: Request to raise our product quantity limit
ticket.raw_subject: Request to raise our product quantity limit
ticket.description:
Hello!

We recently learned that there is a product quantity limit built into your PH Multicarrier Shipping app on Shopify. This is causing issues with our checkout process as we get very large orders about 2-6 times a month that the app doesn't allow through. We are hoping to raise that limit. I also wanted to check if that is a total item quantity limit, or a per product limit?

Please let us know if that is possible!

Thank you,
—Szabi

Szabolcs Kiss
Brand Manager, Californians Together

californianstogether.org (https://californianstogether.org)

525 E. 7th Street, Suite C203 Long Beach, CA 90813  (https://maps.google.com/?q=525+E.+7th+Street,+Suite+C203+Long+Beach,+CA+90813)

comments.body:
Hello Szabi,

Good day!

You are correct—there is currently a limit of 300 quantities per item. If a customer attempts to place an order with more than 300 units of a single item, the rates may fail.
We can certainly increase this limit. Could you please let us know the preferred maximum quantity per item?

Additionally, most of your products are lightweight (around or under 1 lb), while the configured boxes have a maximum weight of 70 lbs. For larger orders (for example, when the maximum quantity is added), could you help us understand how many boxes you would expect the shipment to be split into?

This will help us configure the setup more accurately.

Looking forward to your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:

We can certainly increase this limit. Could you please let us know the preferred maximum quantity per item?
 
Additionally, most of your products are lightweight (around or under 1 lb), while the configured boxes have a maximum weight of 70 lbs. For larger orders (for example, when the maximum quantity is added), could you help us understand how many boxes you would expect the shipment to be split into?
 
This will help us configure the setup more accurately.
 
Looking forward to your response.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello!



Great! If it is per product, I think the maximum number should be 800. We only have a few products we expect to sell that many of at one time, most importantly our Seal of Biliteracy Medallions. I set the max weight at 70lbs (the USPS limit) while I was trying to piece together why our customers were having trouble with their address verification, I now changed them back to 30lbs. In general our items do not weigh enough to reach the weight limits for the boxes, the boxes fill up by volume.



For the medallion product, we regularly receive orders of 100-200 and on occasion orders of 200-800. Technically, based on the 30lb weight limit of the boxes, we can pack 300 of these 0.1lb medallions into a box, so almost all of our orders are shipped in a single box, save for those big ones.



For our publications we regularly receive orders in the hundreds, usually not of a single book though. The largest box we use for these is 11.25x8.75x10". Just as an example: it takes 100 of our 0.1in thick Teacher Education Digests to fill a 10" box, which ends up weighing only around 23lbs. Our High School Counselor Toolkit is 0.43in thick, so only 23 fits in a 10" box, and weighs around 26lbs (once again it caps out due to volume, not weight). So if someone orders 200 of these Counselor toolkits we would ship them in 9 boxes.



Your app has been doing a really good job estimating our shipping costs so far (disregarding the issues with the quantity limit), however we print the labels manually through Shopify because the algorithm creates unnecessarily complex packing (mixing and matching products randomly to optimize boxes). This is fine so far, the app ends up creating roughly the same number of boxes that we do when we stack stuff more logically, and at worst it adds a box which is completely fine.



We just started processing orders, so if anything else comes up I will reach out. Please let me know when the quantity limit is moved up to 800!



Thank you,

—Szabi



 ![photo](https://d36urhup7zbd7q.cloudfront.net/u/MnPgrYPGyo7/5de03012-70f2-48a1-95c7-b3c2d19b131a.png)

Szabolcs Kiss
Brand Manager, Californians Together

![icon](https://gifo.srv.wisestamp.com/s/rfw1/212121/24/trans.png) californianstogether.org

![icon](https://gifo.srv.wisestamp.com/s/rfa2/212121/24/trans.png) 525 E. 7th Street, Suite C203 Long Beach, CA 90813

comments.html_body:
Hello!

Great! If it is per product, I think the maximum number should be 800. We only have a few products we expect to sell that many of at one time, most importantly our Seal of Biliteracy Medallions. I set the max weight at 70lbs (the USPS limit) while I was trying to piece together why our customers were having trouble with their address verification, I now changed them back to 30lbs. In general our items do not weigh enough to reach the weight limits for the boxes, the boxes fill up by volume.

For the medallion product, we regularly receive orders of 100-200 and on occasion orders of 200-800. Technically, based on the 30lb weight limit of the boxes, we can pack 300 of these 0.1lb medallions into a box, so almost all of our orders are shipped in a single box, save for those big ones.

For our publications we regularly receive orders in the hundreds, usually not of a single book though. The largest box we use for these is 11.25x8.75x10". Just as an example: it takes 100 of our 0.1in thick Teacher Education Digests to fill a 10" box, which ends up weighing only around 23lbs. Our High School Counselor Toolkit is 0.43in thick, so only 23 fits in a 10" box, and weighs around 26lbs (once again it caps out due to volume, not weight). So if someone orders 200 of these Counselor toolkits we would ship them in 9 boxes.

Your app has been doing a really good job estimating our shipping costs so far (disregarding the issues with the quantity limit), however we print the labels manually through Shopify because the algorithm creates unnecessarily complex packing (mixing and matching products randomly to optimize boxes). This is fine so far, the app ends up creating roughly the same number of boxes that we do when we stack stuff more logically, and at worst it adds a box which is completely fine.

We just started processing orders, so if anything else comes up I will reach out. Please let me know when the quantity limit is moved up to 800!

Thank you,
—Szabi

Szabolcs Kiss
Brand Manager, Californians Together

525 E. 7th Street, Suite C203 Long Beach, CA 90813

comments.body:
Need assistance from the L3 team on the below case:

**Case:** Increase product quantity limit to 800 per item

**Details:**
The MCSL customer is selling paper posters, medals, and certificates, all of which weigh less than 1 lb. Even if a customer orders up to 800 medals (each weighing ~0.1 lbs), they can be packed within acceptable limits.

The customer is using the box packing method with a maximum weight set to 30 lbs. Even for large orders, the total number of boxes would remain within carrier limits.

**Request:**
Could you please check and confirm if the product quantity limit per item can be increased to 800?

**Store Details:**
Store URL – rfdbdm-du.myshopify.com
Account ID –ba15bb9e-ec48-4485-9967-b64c972a42fa
